name: Junie, the AI coding agent by JetBrains
description:
Junie is an AI coding agent by JetBrains that handles tasks autonomously or in collaboration with a developer. Developers can either fully delegate routine tasks to Junie or partner with it on more complex ones


You think it, Junie makes it happen
Try it out in JetBrains IDEs and join our Discord to stay in touch

What Junie can do for you

Provide a seamless coding experience

Getting started with the AI agent is simple. Install it in your IDE and begin with small tasks that fit seamlessly into your workflow – no dramatic changes required. This approach ensures that your new way of working is empowering, not disruptive, whether you code in Java, Python, JavaScript, Go, etc.

Discover efficient solutions

Junie understands the structure, logic, and relationships in your code. When you delegate a task to the AI agent, it finds the right places to work, the right tools to use, and the best path forward.

Propose an execution plan

Once Junie has explored the project, it will provide a cohesive plan for completing the task. Junie uses Anthropic (Claude Sonnet) and OpenAI (GPT-5) LLMs for the best developer experience, both in coding and reasoning.

Adjust to the task at hand

Use Code mode to execute tasks, with Junie writing and testing the code for you. Switch to Ask mode, to pose questions, collaborate on plans, and brainstorm features and improvements.

Perform inspections you can rely on

When Junie updates your code, it uses the power of your IDE to make sure every change meets your standards. Built-in syntax and semantic checks keep your code clean, consistent, and production-ready. With AI Assistant, code completion, and other features, Junie offers a full AI experience in the IDE that you already trust.

Run tests to keep your project state green

Junie runs code and tests as needed to reduce warnings and compilation errors. After making changes, Junie verifies that everything runs smoothly so that you can focus on building instead of debugging.

Intelligent collaboration on complex tasks

Focus on the big picture while Junie takes care of the routine work. Review, refine, and stay in control. Junie’s here to support you.
notes:
2xx.819.xx

  • Support for OpenAI GPT-5.4
  • CLI improvements: GitHub Actions installation support, /cost and /recap commands, logout command, in-app tips, improved paste handling, Markdown table rendering
  • Added lint tool, web_search tool, rename refactoring, regex search with file extensions
  • Support for reading PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPT, and PPTX files with Markdown conversion
  • Enhanced task state management, history compression, and session replay functionality
  • Added Kotlin to MCP, AGENTS.md support, enhanced MCP server suggestions
  • Support for adaptive thinking for Anthropic models

2xx.620.xx

  • Junie now supports Agent Skills — task-oriented, reusable extensions that allow the agent to perform specialized workflows. To install a skill, add its folder to .junie/skills with a required SKILL.md file used for discovery and execution
  • More efficient edits for non-OpenAI LLMs, now all models are capable of editing several locations in a file within one step
  • Support for guideline directories: instead of a single guidelines file, you can now select a path to a directory with multiple .md files
  • Improved handling of library files
  • Fixed an issue where the hidden plugin could not be reopened; Junie is now accessible from the Tool Windows menu
  • Supported new models:
    • Claude Sonnet 4.6
    • Claude Opus 4.6
    • Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
    • GPT 5.3 Codex

2xx.549.xx

  • Support new models:
    • Gemini 3 Flash — now the default model for Junie
    • OpenAI GPT-5.2
    • xAI Grok 4.1 Fast (Reasoning)
  • Support “Bring Your Own Key” (BYOK) for OpenAI and Anthropic in the AI Chat interface
  • Faster LLM responses due to infra-related improvements
  • More stable and accurate user language detection with fewer accidental switches
  • Security and bug fixes via dependency updates

2xx.506.xx

  • Support of OpenAI GPT-5.1-Codex-Max
  • Various technical improvements and bug fixes

2xx.487.xx

  • Junie now supports multilingual responses
  • The Allowlist is now more flexible: you can individually allow Terminal (including RegEx patterns), MCP, Build, RunTest, and other actions; see the Junie documentation for details
  • Support new models:
    • GPT-5.1
    • Gemini 3.0
    • Claude Opus 4.5
  • Support for 2025.3 IDE version
  • Deprecated support for 2025.1 IDE version
  • Various technical improvements and bug fixes

2xx.406.xx

  • Added "Auto" mode that lets Junie choose between coding and chatting flows automatically
  • MCP remote servers can now be configured (Beta):
    • URL servers without auth, local trusted servers, HTTP headers auth
    • SSO authorization is not supported
  • Customizable guidelines path per project
  • Asynchronous terminal runner – Junie can launch never‑ending processes in isolated shells (e.g., 'npm start')
  • Rename Refactoring tool added
  • Junie now has the "Default" model setting – stay on the model that is regularly updated to the one performing best, according to our evaluations
  • Sunsetting older Claude models (Sonnet 3.7, 4.0)
  • Changes to edit listener
  • Various improvements to task view animations, layouts, and scroll behavior

2xx.336.xx

  • Latency improvements and reasoning optimizations for GPT-5
  • Multi-language support
  • Support for CLion (Beta, IDE 2025.2 only)
  • Asynchronous terminal initialization
  • Added Continue block when Junie stops due to errors or reaching the step limit
  • Improved image handling, Junie can now open images
  • Deprecated support for 2024.3 IDE version
  • Support of Claude Sonnet 4.5

2xx.284.xx

  • Support for Rider (Beta, IDE 2025.2 only)

2xx.264.xx

  • GPT-5 is now the default LLM for Junie
  • Support for images in prompts
  • Junie Asks Questions feature: Junie will now ask for your advice when needed
  • PyCharm Community and PyCharm Unified Free support

2xx.204.xx

  • Beta MCP support (only text mode, SSE not yet supported)
  • WSL support
  • Beta Remote Development Support (Windows not yet supported)
  • 30% faster agent execution, Think More option for more complex tasks
  • Ask Mode response is rendered as Markdown
  • Junie terminal commands are no longer saved to terminal history

2xx.167.xx

  • Single-column plan layout
  • Compatibility with IDEA Community
  • Optional support for Sonnet 4.0
  • Improved license management with usage alerts and license selection dialog
  • New notification system
  • Explicit terminal initialization step

2xx.132.xx

  • Android Studio compatibility added (requires IDE version Narwhal 2025.1.1 Canary or newer)
  • Improved response times by migrating some operations to asynchronous processing
  • Better tracking of file changes during shell command execution
  • Title summarization added for reports in "Ask" mode
  • Implemented build script change detection system for improved IDE integration

2xx.105.40

  • Support for RubyMine (IDE 2025.1 only), PhpStorm, and RustRover
  • Support PowerShell 7
  • Junie's project directory can now be configured via settings
  • .aiignore to restrict Junie's access to specific files and directories
  • Flexible Brave mode: configurable allow‑list of commands with RegEx support
  • UI/UX fixes and polish
